review: This might be a hot take, but I had an absolute blast playing this game. Yeah, the pacing may be rough and slow a lot of the time, but I was so invested in the story and characters that I really didn’t notice it all that much.

I do not get how this how this is 77 I have not beat the game yet and I have had aleast a 92  this game has some much flavour in the bros attacks the story is also amazing its reall theme is just perfect and a must play Mario rpg.
Mario And Luigi Brothership was amazing and I love to play as how they workedtogether.
A game that is as long as is equally boring. The best part is the few mario rpg aspects of battle. Most missions are strung out far too long for no real reason the game is not really fun.

This game really delivers on finally bringing back this series with some drawbacks. I really like some of the characters, the UI is pretty nice looking and reminds me of how 3DS uis looked. I just wish the level up screen was more soulful but everything else is great. The music is pretty good if a bit forgettable, The rpgs mechanics are amazing in this one and the devs allow you to really go wild with different builds, I love the character variety though I do wish mushroom kingdom characters had more variance with looks and costumes. the game also can be challenging especially towards the end. The only big flaws is the weird framerate dips which feel un-mario and also the pacing. The game would've been perfect if it ended when you defeated Zokkett. But the extra stuff in the end was kinda anti-climatic.

Overall, great game and one of my favorite RPGS on switch but it kinda overstays it's welcome. All this game needs is better pacing.
A good return to form for this franchise but way longer than it should be. The animation and battle system is great in this game though:

Good game overall, but sadly not fun enough to justify the huge time investment. (We’re talking 50+ hours for the main story + side quests.) Combat often feels boring and repetitive—there’s no nuance or strategy, 99% of the time you just spam your strongest move (which have agonisingly long, unskippable animations by the way. These get longer and longer as you unlock more powerful moves) and wait for it to be over. Sidequests include the usual offenders of fetch quests, walking simulator quests, and, in a couple of cases, loot grinding. Which is a shame because the story is genuinely interesting. The game does have a good soul, it’s just hampered by boring, repetitive combat and generic sidequests that pad the length out far longer than it needed to be.

REVIEW: EXCELLENT GAME ️At first, I thought it would be boring — I even started to regret my purchase. But soon the game completely hooked me! I realized it’s not just about RPG battles; every move makes you think, but in a fun and engaging way. The best part is the story — just when you think the game is about to end… BOOM!  It surprises you!I found it to be a really great game, I totally recommend it. 5 stars ️

Mario & Luigi: Brotherhood Connection recovers the spirit of the series with its humor, dynamic combat, and cooperative mechanics, but it fails to surprise or reinvent itself. Although it is an entertaining adventure, the jump to 3D subtracts some of the visual charm, the music goes unnoticed, and the difficulty is too low to pose a real challenge.Furthermore, performance issues can affect fluidity in key moments. It's not a bad game, but it's not a memorable return either; fans will enjoy it, but it leaves the feeling that it could have been much more.

1. The graphic is a more simplistic cell-shading style which I'm not sure if I like. Still, I can say it does weird me out sometimes and it REALLY bothers me that Mario and Luigi have this weird blue tint on their clothes. 2. Fighting is good but it annoys that you have little ways to avoid  battles with weak enemies and it's still round based. If you don't like it, this game won't do anything to change your mind. Eventually you also will end up to constantly using the strongest brother attacks and skill and equip your characters to optimize their use, just to get quicker through the many battles you can hardly avoid, not matter if the enemy is strong or weak. 3. Each of the major bosses has unique abilities and mechanics and if you haven't mastered the dodge mechanic facing them, you will have a bad time. It is fun how different they are however. 4. On different islands you have different actions and gimmicks to go through to finish it and it's nice and varied so it never overstays it's welcome but some of them can get real infuriating, especially on subsequent visits for completion. One especially enraging is the rhythm mini-game that keeps on getting worse and it gave me a real bad time but it might just be me that has an issue with it but it was bad enough for me to drop the rating one extra point. 5. The “plug” system works nice at first, allows to adapt to situation and is just rightly restricted  but as you craft more of them, it becomes a bit redundant because the way you end up playing and the enemies you face on the regular becomes narrow and the recharge is a bit long and some plugs you hardly ever use, only as a “just to have something until it recharges” case. 6. Overall it's a pretty good new entry of the series and a good return but it has a few issues and doesn't revolve or evolve the series but also doesn't do anything wrong so there is no reason to avoid it, unless you hate the kind of game it is.

Nintendo really likes bringing back old things just for nostalgia. However, I've never played a Mario&Luigi game before this one so I'm not gonna be driven by emotions. First things first, it looks really nice, I like the new artstyle, it's refreshing. The combat system is really fun and I find myself going towards enemies just to play battles. The story is great, I like the plot twist at the end and it's overall a pretty good story for a Mario game. I wanted togive Mario & Luigi: Brothership a 9, but I think that towards the end of the game the pacing starts to slow down and you get bored of esploring and just playing. All in all, I think it's one of the best Mario RPG experiences you can buy on the Switch thanks to its presentation, story and beatiful mechanics

A game oozing with charm that overstays its welcome several times. The overall theming is very nice, and the fun gameplay and lovely soundtrack help it out a little.
Great game and an amazing return to the franchise, the only drawback is the slow progression of the powerups.

It's been overwhelming. I liked the visuals and most of the gameplay but the game is too unbalanced and drawn out in time for my likings. And that freaky song in EVERY battle... Could have used several different ones...

The beginning is amazing, the middle of it is tiring and then the ending goes back to amazing. I liked the story and connected to the characters, but midgame has some really boring moments. Still recommend it though, I had a nice time with it!
Way too much hand-holding. This game is targeted to the same audience as Dora the Explorer, and fails even with them because no one on earth wants to read endless pointless dialogue.

Brothership is a solid entry into the Mario & Luigi series with fun combat, an intriguing (if somewhat contrived) storyline, and relatively satisfying boss battles and secret-hunting elements. However, it lacks the strong, idiosyncratic humor from previous iterations like "Partners In Time" or "Bowser's Inside Story", the difficulty level leaves a lot to be desired, and there is little-to-no incentive to replay the game. 

I did not take any damage until the first boss, and I did not have either of the Bros faint until 12-13 hours into the game, which was easily remedied by one of the dozen-or-so 1-UPs I had accumulated thus far. I didn't once get a "GAME OVER" during my playthrough. Mushrooms were more of a sellable commodity than a useful mid-battle heal, and the defensive gear options seemed inconsequential when I wasn't taking any hits in the first place. I understand this may not have been the typical experience, but, at least for me, the game felt way too easy. It's okay to make the game accessible to fans that are less experienced with RPGs, some difficulty options (sliders, toggles, really ANYTHING) for veterans like myself would have been welcomed with open arms.

The story this time around was acceptable, but little more than that. It also felt somewhat contrived, and it crucially lacked some of the punchy, oddball humor that other games in the series had plenty of. The key side characters felt more like plot devices than actual living, breathing elements that genuinely interacted with the Bros, and the world was little more than a series of locations designed to advance the story. Each new island offered little in the way of unique storytelling elements; one by one, Mario and Luigi would travel there, plug in the giant plug, and leave. Very few of the "levels" left a lasting impact on me, and I felt no desire (outside of the requirement for sidequests) to revisit any of them.

All this to say, the game ISN'T BAD. Likewise, however, it's not much more than DECENT. In some ways, it feels half-baked (particularly the story and character development), and I don't see myself coming back to this one a couple of years down the road to revisit the story or re-explore the islands. Fans had to wait a long time for this game, and it's a bit disappointing that Nintendo couldn't deliver a more fulfilling, engaging experience. Add a difficulty slider or hard mode, and I'd be all over it, but until then I won't be returning to Concordia any time soon. I'd give it a 7.4 if I could, but I suppose a 7 is closer to what I'm feeling here than an 8.
Boring game and a lot talking make the game more boring the gameplay in not that bad i dont like the style of gameplay

Annoyingly long. Mario & Luigi games do not need to be 50+ hours long. The writing is good, but not good enough to warrant how much of it there is, has to be, due to how long this friggin game is. I had a mission whose description was something along the lines of readying myself for the final fight,  got to and beat said fight, and WHOOPS, here's at least two more set pieces we want you to run through.  The gameplay is fine, but again, not varied enough to hold up this amount of content, and the rate at which you get upgrades and abilities is criminally slow. If this game was 2/3rds the length it is,  it would be much better.

It's been more than a half a year since I've got this game, still not finished it. Every time I open my save file I can't help but feel like I am wasting my time. Pacing is genuinely awful, dialogues are too chatty, new characters are not interesting at all, side missions are mere fetch quests. This game is such a wasted potential from previous Mario & Luigi games.

One of the worst games in the series. Outdated gameplay, boring characters, annoying voice sfx, amateurish graphics, and too much text. Cutscenes interrupt every two steps. Slow pacing. I liked this game, I felt that it was too long but it was very entertained. Don't get me wrong the game was pretty good I just don't like to spend too much time playing a game. I highly recommend it but if you want to pick a short game this isn't for you.

Mario & Luigi: Brothership is a solid addition to the franchise, offering a fun and nostalgic experience with innovations that refresh the gameplay. Despite some minor performance issues and a difficulty level that could be higher for experienced players, the game stands out for its visual presentation, music, and game mechanics.

Mario and Luigi: Brothership Review — 6.7/10

Mario and Luigi: Brothership feels like that one group project where everyone had good ideas, but no one really finished their part. The base gameplay is fine — battling is fun enough to keep you awake, but it’s nothing you’ll be telling your friends about over dinner. For a Mario & Luigi game, it’s surprisingly... mid.

The biggest problem? Repetition. After a while, it feels like you're doing the same thing over and over, like being stuck on an endless treadmill of "pretty good" ideas that never really go anywhere. It's like the game wanted to be a grand adventure, but someone hit "save and exit" halfway through development.

The story and concept are actually decent, and you can tell there was passion behind this. But it’s hard to shake the feeling that Brothership plays more like a demo for a bigger, better game that never showed up.

In the end, it's not terrible. It's not great. It's just okay. Hardcore fans might enjoy it, but casual players might wonder why they didn’t just replay an older Mario & Luigi game instead.

Final Verdict: Like getting a free sample at the store — tastes good, but not enough to buy the full product

Pros:A highly enjoyable, old-school turn-based RPG experience.Epic and innovative boss battles that keep players engaged.Outstanding soundtrack and sound effects that enhance the overall atmosphere.
Cons: Many side quests lack excitement and feel monotonous.Regular combat becomes somewhat repetitive after approximately 30 hours of gameplay.

Mario & Luigi: Brothership was an interesting game to say the least. It had many good qualities but also quite a few bad ones. For context this was my first Mario & Luigi game, so I hadn’t played any prior. Highlights of the game include its story, characters and overarching mechanics (linking islands and Luigi logic). I thought it provided a charming and wholesome story with amazing characters to support it, I especially liked Connie and Technikki. I found exploring and linking new islands to be extremely enjoyable and a fun mechanic. However one major downfall of this game is how long it went on for, it took me 40+ hours to complete no Mario and Luigi title should take that long to finish and at many parts it felt like the game would never end and it was a chore to play. Another small thing that very much annoyed me was the STUPID tree animation that would play after literally anything and everything, it did not need to be there it added absolutely nothing and consistently wasted my time. But overall, Mario & Luigi: Brothership is a solid game with subtle flaws. As long as you push through some of the boring bits you’ll have an enjoyable experience.

Fun story, fun mechanics, but one issue i HATE! Its that its not 2-player co-op. I saw the trailer and was like, "cool a game i can play with my brother." WRONG! this is single player and u control both! What!

Now, im not saying its a BAD game! It has an excellent story, super fun mechanics, and i LOVE rpg's. If u just want a cool game for you, and dont have any friends, its lovely. But if u were expecting a cooperative multiplayer game then you prolly should not buy!

I want to rate this much, much higher, because by the time I beat this game I was very happy with it. But the first 80% of the game was a slog.

If you can stick it through to the last act, its among the best of the best of Mario and Luigi.
phenomenal game. absolute masterpiece. boss battles were good the amount of character depth was even better. I teared up a little at the end. would recommend

this game was so bad I gave up after the first main lighthouse. this was the most boring nothing game I've ever played. Luigi does way to much and it's shoved into your face whenever luigi has something he can do. the character designs are all bland and boring and all look the same.  leveling up is something that just happens and it doesn't feel like your getting that much stronger. it also never feels like you actually have to but any items at all. the battle system has way to much unnecessary stuff thrown into it. they decided to go for quantity over quality for all the islands and it shows. it feels like nintendo was trying to use the hype and another mario and luigi game to sell this one but it just didn't work. IGN was right.

hello my dear viewers, happy mario day (well actually happy mario week because mario day has already passed). but anyway, for the mario week special i'm gonna review the return to the greatest RPGS in videogame history: Mario and Luigi Brothership. now unlike most of my reviews where i comment about the flaws and positives, i will talk about IGN's review of this game. there's a conspiracy going on surrounding IGN about this game, i might aswell check the review to figure out the conspiracy. This is a return to a great RPG series, so we can assume that IGN is gonna give this game a.... 5?! only a 5?! The new mario and luigi game just got it's ### handed to it by IGN! well thats fine, because i will keep chasing down IGN with my pitchfork and torch. see IGN is known for their reviews, they rate games depending on how they feel, telling people to avoid games that are bad, but instead most of those reviews are complete lies! like concord, dragon age veilguard and this game. this review damaged the game's reputation, so i'm gonna fix it, by reviewing ign's review about this game. the first flaw that IGN said is that it's the worst mario RPG game on the switch, or infact the worst mario game on the switch because it got the lowest score of all the mario games on the switch. i don't think it's the worst one, what i consider the mario game to be the worst of them all is Mario Strikers Battle League, and i already told you how bad that game is. but IGN just gave that game an 8, but this game they gave it a 5, that doesn't make any sense! second flaw is the sailing in this game, IGN said that he didn't like it because it's not wind waker. just because it's not like in wind waker doesn't mean it's bad.  like who the f### cares? we all like the sea exploration no matter if it's wind waker-ish or not (actually i do, but only at the beginning, because the ship moves so slow that i will be old man by the time i'll get to a new island, but once you get a way to speed the ship up, it's not a problem). 3rd: IGN said that this is "babies' first rpg". what's made for babies? is it the difficulty? the exploration? either way, it's made for everyone hence the rating "rated E for everybody". 4th: handholding dialogue. according to IGN, the dialogues keep holding your hand again and again throughout the game. but really, Why is Logan Plant having so much trouble skipping the cutscenes? He doesn't know he can speed up the dialogue by holding the B button, The reviewer **** at skipping cutscenes more so than any human being regardless of age or experience could possibly just forget the skip button. the irony just baffles me. 5th: the story. IGN apparently just hates the story because the writing is unoriginal. okay, the writing for me is just perfection. trust me, the writing is great and original, just don't listen to him. 6th: the characters. okay this is just ridiculous. IGN didn't like the characters from this game, let alone original characters. remember when alot of people hated Sticker Star? isn't it pretty clear that Original characters is important for RPGs? 7th: generic locations. most of the islands you visit are mostly ice, fire, sand, etc. it doesn't even count as generic, it's the same aesthetics over and over again. how many settings could you think of other than grass, fire or ice? 8th: perfomance issue. look i'm using an OLED model of the switch, and the only performance issue i could find was the loading screen. because how does this affect the whole game anyway? it's not like the Glitch Gremlin f##### up this game's performance more so than in Pokemon Scarlet and Violet, so the game is running smooth. 9th: Luigi works differently. this is the only IGN complaint i agree on, because Luigi works different here, in most mario and luigi games he follows you around and can be a second character you control, but here, it's like there's a second player controlling him. he follows you at an awkward angle, he can jump on his own, and to top it off, you can't control him by swapping with the "-" button. okay this is the last complaint about ign in this review: the pacing. he said that the third act added nothing of substance. but i don't feel disappointed about it. because it's one of the best third acts in videogame history. on par with Marvel's Spider Man's 1 and 2 act 3. alright that's all the flaws i could mention about ign's review of this game, this rating might have hurt the game, but i fixed it with this review, anyways, don't listen to what ign says, just listen to what i say in these metacritic scores, anyway, hope this review fixed the reputation and forget about the ign rating of this game, just take it and throw it away. anyways i consider this game to be the 2nd best RPG game on the switch, well i'm running out of characters so i might aswell end the review right here, so... peace out. 9/10

The biggest mixed bag **** I've played in a long while. Generally its positives are better than its negatives, but man it's kinda hard to tell whether it's a Mario & Luigi game or a Mario game (ft. Luigi). Still, those positives will stick in my mind for a long while, including some characters that I really can see becoming fan favorites.

With “Mario & Luigi: Brothership,” Nintendo once again delivered a great role-playing game with interesting gameplay aspects and humorous dialogues. The graphic representation, the playful depth in the fights and the loving presentation created a great atmosphere and an excellent impression for us. Our highlight of last year and fans of the predecessors and newcomers should definitely buy “Mario & Luigi: Brothership”.

Good game overall, but sadly not fun enough to justify the huge time investment. (We’re talking 50+ hours for the main story + side quests.) Combat often feels boring and repetitive—there’s no nuance or strategy, 99% of the time you just spam your strongest move (which have agonisingly long, unskippable animations by the way. These get longer and longer as you unlock more powerful moves) and wait for it to be over. Sidequests include the usual offenders of fetch quests, walking simulator quests, and, in a couple of cases, loot grinding. Which is a shame because the story is genuinely interesting. The game does have a good soul, it’s just hampered by boring, repetitive combat and generic sidequests that pad the length out far longer than it needed to be.

Mario & Luigi: Brotherhood Connection recovers the spirit of the series with its humor, dynamic combat, and cooperative mechanics, but it fails to surprise or reinvent itself. Although it is an entertaining adventure, the jump to 3D subtracts some of the visual charm, the music goes unnoticed, and the difficulty is too low to pose a real challenge.Furthermore, performance issues can affect fluidity in key moments. It's not a bad game, but it's not a memorable return either; fans will enjoy it, but it leaves the feeling that it could have been much more.

1. The graphic is a more simplistic cell-shading style which I'm not sure if I like. Still, I can say it does weird me out sometimes and it REALLY bothers me that Mario and Luigi have this weird blue tint on their clothes. 2. Fighting is good but it annoys that you have little ways to avoid  battles with weak enemies and it's still round based. If you don't like it, this game won't do anything to change your mind. Eventually you also will end up to constantly using the strongest brother attacks and skill and equip your characters to optimize their use, just to get quicker through the many battles you can hardly avoid, not matter if the enemy is strong or weak. 3. Each of the major bosses has unique abilities and mechanics and if you haven't mastered the dodge mechanic facing them, you will have a bad time. It is fun how different they are however. 4. On different islands you have different actions and gimmicks to go through to finish it and it's nice and varied so it never overstays it's welcome but some of them can get real infuriating, especially on subsequent visits for completion. One especially enraging is the rhythm mini-game that keeps on getting worse and it gave me a real bad time but it might just be me that has an issue with it but it was bad enough for me to drop the rating one extra point. 5. The “plug” system works nice at first, allows to adapt to situation and is just rightly restricted  but as you craft more of them, it becomes a bit redundant because the way you end up playing and the enemies you face on the regular becomes narrow and the recharge is a bit long and some plugs you hardly ever use, only as a “just to have something until it recharges” case. 6. Overall it's a pretty good new entry of the series and a good return but it has a few issues and doesn't revolve or evolve the series but also doesn't do anything wrong so there is no reason to avoid it, unless you hate the kind of game it is.

Nintendo really likes bringing back old things just for nostalgia. However, I've never played a Mario&Luigi game before this one so I'm not gonna be driven by emotions. I wanted togive Mario & Luigi: Brothership a 9, but I think that towards the end of the game the pacing starts to slow down and you get bored of esploring and just playing. All in all, I think it's one of the best Mario RPG experiences you can buy on the Switch thanks to its presentation, story and beatiful mechanics

Brothership is a solid entry into the Mario & Luigi series with fun combat, an intriguing (if somewhat contrived) storyline, and relatively satisfying boss battles and secret-hunting elements. However, it lacks the strong, idiosyncratic humor from previous iterations like "Partners In Time" or "Bowser's Inside Story", the difficulty level leaves a lot to be desired, and there is little-to-no incentive to replay the game. 

I did not take any damage until the first boss, and I did not have either of the Bros faint until 12-13 hours into the game, which was easily remedied by one of the dozen-or-so 1-UPs I had accumulated thus far. I didn't once get a "GAME OVER" during my playthrough. Mushrooms were more of a sellable commodity than a useful mid-battle heal, and the defensive gear options seemed inconsequential when I wasn't taking any hits in the first place. I understand this may not have been the typical experience, but, at least for me, the game felt way too easy. It's okay to make the game accessible to fans that are less experienced with RPGs, some difficulty options (sliders, toggles, really ANYTHING) for veterans like myself would have been welcomed with open arms.

The story this time around was acceptable, but little more than that. It also felt somewhat contrived, and it crucially lacked some of the punchy, oddball humor that other games in the series had plenty of. The key side characters felt more like plot devices than actual living, breathing elements that genuinely interacted with the Bros, and the world was little more than a series of locations designed to advance the story. Each new island offered little in the way of unique storytelling elements; one by one, Mario and Luigi would travel there, plug in the giant plug, and leave. Very few of the "levels" left a lasting impact on me, and I felt no desire (outside of the requirement for sidequests) to revisit any of them.

All this to say, the game ISN'T BAD. Likewise, however, it's not much more than DECENT. In some ways, it feels half-baked (particularly the story and character development), and I don't see myself coming back to this one a couple of years down the road to revisit the story or re-explore the islands. Fans had to wait a long time for this game, and it's a bit disappointing that Nintendo couldn't deliver a more fulfilling, engaging experience. Add a difficulty slider or hard mode, and I'd be all over it, but until then I won't be returning to Concordia any time soon. I'd give it a 7.4 if I could, but I suppose a 7 is closer to what I'm feeling here than an 8.

Annoyingly long. Mario & Luigi games do not need to be 50+ hours long. The writing is good, but not good enough to warrant how much of it there is, has to be, due to how long this friggin game is. I had a mission whose description was something along the lines of readying myself for the final fight,  got to and beat said fight, and WHOOPS, here's at least two more set pieces we want you to run through.  The gameplay is fine, but again, not varied enough to hold up this amount of content, and the rate at which you get upgrades and abilities is criminally slow. If this game was 2/3rds the length it is,  it would be much better.

It's been more than a half a year since I've got this game, still not finished it. Every time I open my save file I can't help but feel like I am wasting my time. Pacing is genuinely awful, dialogues are too chatty, new characters are not interesting at all, side missions are mere fetch quests. This game is such a wasted potential from previous Mario & Luigi games.

One of the worst games in the series. Outdated gameplay, boring characters, annoying voice sfx, amateurish graphics, and too much text. Cutscenes interrupt every two steps. Slow pacing. No offense, but this is one of Nintendo's worst games. I've tried it several times now, but I just can't get excited about the game. I hope the next game will be better, I actually like this series.

I liked this game, I felt that it was too long but it was very entertained. Don't get me wrong the game was pretty good I just don't like to spend too much time playing a game. I highly recommend it but if you want to pick a short game this isn't for you.

Mario & Luigi: Brothership is a solid addition to the franchise, offering a fun and nostalgic experience with innovations that refresh the gameplay. Despite some minor performance issues and a difficulty level that could be higher for experienced players, the game stands out for its visual presentation, music, and game mechanics.

Mario and Luigi: Brothership Review — 6.7/10

Mario and Luigi: Brothership feels like that one group project where everyone had good ideas, but no one really finished their part. The base gameplay is fine — battling is fun enough to keep you awake, but it’s nothing you’ll be telling your friends about over dinner. For a Mario & Luigi game, it’s surprisingly... mid.

The biggest problem? Repetition. After a while, it feels like you're doing the same thing over and over, like being stuck on an endless treadmill of "pretty good" ideas that never really go anywhere. It's like the game wanted to be a grand adventure, but someone hit "save and exit" halfway through development.

The story and concept are actually decent, and you can tell there was passion behind this. But it’s hard to shake the feeling that Brothership plays more like a demo for a bigger, better game that never showed up.

In the end, it's not terrible. It's not great. It's just okay. Hardcore fans might enjoy it, but casual players might wonder why they didn’t just replay an older Mario & Luigi game instead.

Final Verdict: Like getting a free sample at the store — tastes good, but not enough to buy the full product

Pros:A highly enjoyable, old-school turn-based RPG experience.Epic and innovative boss battles that keep players engaged.Outstanding soundtrack and sound effects that enhance the overall atmosphere.
Cons: Many side quests lack excitement and feel monotonous.Regular combat becomes somewhat repetitive after approximately 30 hours of gameplay.

Mario & Luigi: Brothership was an interesting game to say the least. It had many good qualities but also quite a few bad ones. For context this was my first Mario & Luigi game, so I hadn’t played any prior. Highlights of the game include its story, characters and overarching mechanics (linking islands and Luigi logic). I thought it provided a charming and wholesome story with amazing characters to support it, I especially liked Connie and Technikki. I found exploring and linking new islands to be extremely enjoyable and a fun mechanic. However one major downfall of this game is how long it went on for, it took me 40+ hours to complete no Mario and Luigi title should take that long to finish and at many parts it felt like the game would never end and it was a chore to play. Another small thing that very much annoyed me was the STUPID tree animation that would play after literally anything and everything, it did not need to be there it added absolutely nothing and consistently wasted my time. But overall, Mario & Luigi: Brothership is a solid game with subtle flaws. As long as you push through some of the boring bits you’ll have an enjoyable experience.

Fun story, fun mechanics, but one issue i HATE! Its that its not 2-player co-op. I saw the trailer and was like, "cool a game i can play with my brother." WRONG! this is single player and u control both! What!

Now, im not saying its a BAD game! It has an excellent story, super fun mechanics, and i LOVE rpg's. If u just want a cool game for you, and dont have any friends, its lovely. But if u were expecting a cooperative multiplayer game then you prolly should not buy!

I want to rate this much, much higher, because by the time I beat this game I was very happy with it. But the first 80% of the game was a slog.

If you can stick it through to the last act, its among the best of the best of Mario and Luigi.
phenomenal game. absolute masterpiece. boss battles were good the amount of character depth was even better. I teared up a little at the end. would recommend

this game was so bad I gave up after the first main lighthouse. this was the most boring nothing game I've ever played. Luigi does way to much and it's shoved into your face whenever luigi has something he can do. the character designs are all bland and boring and all look the same.  leveling up is something that just happens and it doesn't feel like your getting that much stronger. it also never feels like you actually have to but any items at all. the battle system has way to much unnecessary stuff thrown into it. they decided to go for quantity over quality for all the islands and it shows. it feels like nintendo was trying to use the hype and another mario and luigi game to sell this one but it just didn't work. IGN was right.

hello my dear viewers, happy mario day (well actually happy mario week because mario day has already passed). but anyway, for the mario week special i'm gonna review the return to the greatest RPGS in videogame history: Mario and Luigi Brothership. now unlike most of my reviews where i comment about the flaws and positives, i will talk about IGN's review of this game. there's a conspiracy going on surrounding IGN about this game, i might aswell check the review to figure out the conspiracy. This is a return to a great RPG series, so we can assume that IGN is gonna give this game a.... 5?! only a 5?! The new mario and luigi game just got it's ### handed to it by IGN! well thats fine, because i will keep chasing down IGN with my pitchfork and torch. see IGN is known for their reviews, they rate games depending on how they feel, telling people to avoid games that are bad, but instead most of those reviews are complete lies! like concord, dragon age veilguard and this game. this review damaged the game's reputation, so i'm gonna fix it, by reviewing ign's review about this game. the first flaw that IGN said is that it's the worst mario RPG game on the switch, or infact the worst mario game on the switch because it got the lowest score of all the mario games on the switch. i don't think it's the worst one, what i consider the mario game to be the worst of them all is Mario Strikers Battle League, and i already told you how bad that game is. but IGN just gave that game an 8, but this game they gave it a 5, that doesn't make any sense! second flaw is the sailing in this game, IGN said that he didn't like it because it's not wind waker. just because it's not like in wind waker doesn't mean it's bad.  like who the f### cares? we all like the sea exploration no matter if it's wind waker-ish or not (actually i do, but only at the beginning, because the ship moves so slow that i will be old man by the time i'll get to a new island, but once you get a way to speed the ship up, it's not a problem). 3rd: IGN said that this is "babies' first rpg". what's made for babies? is it the difficulty? the exploration? either way, it's made for everyone hence the rating "rated E for everybody". 4th: handholding dialogue. according to IGN, the dialogues keep holding your hand again and again throughout the game. but really, Why is Logan Plant having so much trouble skipping the cutscenes? He doesn't know he can speed up the dialogue by holding the B button, The reviewer **** at skipping cutscenes more so than any human being regardless of age or experience could possibly just forget the skip button. the irony just baffles me. 5th: the story. IGN apparently just hates the story because the writing is unoriginal. okay, the writing for me is just perfection. trust me, the writing is great and original, just don't listen to him. 6th: the characters. okay this is just ridiculous. IGN didn't like the characters from this game, let alone original characters. remember when alot of people hated Sticker Star? isn't it pretty clear that Original characters is important for RPGs? 7th: generic locations. most of the islands you visit are mostly ice, fire, sand, etc. it doesn't even count as generic, it's the same aesthetics over and over again. how many settings could you think of other than grass, fire or ice? 8th: perfomance issue. look i'm using an OLED model of the switch, and the only performance issue i could find was the loading screen. because how does this affect the whole game anyway? it's not like the Glitch Gremlin f##### up this game's performance more so than in Pokemon Scarlet and Violet, so the game is running smooth. 9th: Luigi works differently. this is the only IGN complaint i agree on, because Luigi works different here, in most mario and luigi games he follows you around and can be a second character you control, but here, it's like there's a second player controlling him. he follows you at an awkward angle, he can jump on his own, and to top it off, you can't control him by swapping with the "-" button. okay this is the last complaint about ign in this review: the pacing. he said that the third act added nothing of substance. but i don't feel disappointed about it. because it's one of the best third acts in videogame history. on par with Marvel's Spider Man's 1 and 2 act 3. alright that's all the flaws i could mention about ign's review of this game, this rating might have hurt the game, but i fixed it with this review, anyways, don't listen to what ign says, just listen to what i say in these metacritic scores, anyway, hope this review fixed the reputation and forget about the ign rating of this game, just take it and throw it away. anyways i consider this game to be the 2nd best RPG game on the switch, well i'm running out of characters so i might aswell end the review right here, so... peace out. 9/10

Luigi). Still, those positives will stick in my mind for a long while, including some characters that I really can see becoming fan favorites.

With “Mario & Luigi: Brothership,” Nintendo once again delivered a great role-playing game with interesting gameplay aspects and humorous dialogues. The graphic representation, the playful depth in the fights and the loving presentation created a great atmosphere and an excellent impression for us. Our highlight of last year and fans of the predecessors and newcomers should definitely buy “Mario & Luigi: Brothership”.

This might be a hot take, but I had an absolute blast playing this game. Yeah, the pacing may be rough and slow a lot of the time, but I was so invested in the story and characters that I really didn’t notice it all that much.

This game really delivers on finally bringing back this series with some drawbacks. I really like some of the characters, the UI is pretty nice looking and reminds me of how 3DS uis looked. I just wish the level up screen was more soulful but everything else is great. The music is pretty good if a bit forgettable, The rpgs mechanics are amazing in this one and the devs allow you to really go wild with different builds, I love the character variety though I do wish mushroom kingdom characters had more variance with looks and costumes. the game also can be challenging especially towards the end. The only big flaws is the weird framerate dips which feel un-mario and also the pacing. The game would've been perfect if it ended when you defeated Zokkett. But the extra stuff in the end was kinda anti-climatic.

Overall, great game and one of my favorite RPGS on switch but it kinda overstays it's welcome. All this game needs is better pacing.
A good return to form for this franchise but way longer than it should be. The animation and battle system is great in this game though:

Good game overall, but sadly not fun enough to justify the huge time investment. (We’re talking 50+ hours for the main story + side quests.) Combat often feels boring and repetitive—there’s no nuance or strategy, 99% of the time you just spam your strongest move (which have agonisingly long, unskippable animations by the way. These get longer and longer as you unlock more powerful moves) and wait for it to be over. Sidequests include the usual offenders of fetch quests, walking simulator quests, and, in a couple of cases, loot grinding. Which is a shame because the story is genuinely interesting. The game does have a good soul, it’s just hampered by boring, repetitive combat and generic sidequests that pad the length out far longer than it needed to be.

REVIEW: EXCELLENT GAME ️At first, I thought it would be boring — I even started to regret my purchase. But soon the game completely hooked me! I realized it’s not just about RPG battles; every move makes you think, but in a fun and engaging way. The best part is the story — just when you think the game is about to end… BOOM!  It surprises you!I found it to be a really great game, I totally recommend it. 5 stars ️

Mario & Luigi: Brotherhood Connection recovers the spirit of the series with its humor, dynamic combat, and cooperative mechanics, but it fails to surprise or reinvent itself. Although it is an entertaining adventure, the jump to 3D subtracts some of the visual charm, the music goes unnoticed, and the difficulty is too low to pose a real challenge.Furthermore, performance issues can affect fluidity in key moments. It's not a bad game, but it's not a memorable return either; fans will enjoy it, but it leaves the feeling that it could have been much more.

1. The graphic is a more simplistic cell-shading style which I'm not sure if I like. Still, I can say it does weird me out sometimes and it REALLY bothers me that Mario and Luigi have this weird blue tint on their clothes. 2. Fighting is good but it annoys that you have little ways to avoid  battles with weak enemies and it's still round based. If you don't like it, this game won't do anything to change your mind. Eventually you also will end up to constantly using the strongest brother attacks and skill and equip your characters to optimize their use, just to get quicker through the many battles you can hardly avoid, not matter if the enemy is strong or weak. 3. Each of the major bosses has unique abilities and mechanics and if you haven't mastered the dodge mechanic facing them, you will have a bad time. It is fun how different they are however. 4. On different islands you have different actions and gimmicks to go through to finish it and it's nice and varied so it never overstays it's welcome but some of them can get real infuriating, especially on subsequent visits for completion. One especially enraging is the rhythm mini-game that keeps on getting worse and it gave me a real bad time but it might just be me that has an issue with it but it was bad enough for me to drop the rating one extra point. 5. The “plug” system works nice at first, allows to adapt to situation and is just rightly restricted  but as you craft more of them, it becomes a bit redundant because the way you end up playing and the enemies you face on the regular becomes narrow and the recharge is a bit long and some plugs you hardly ever use, only as a “just to have something until it recharges” case. 6. Overall it's a pretty good new entry of the series and a good return but it has a few issues and doesn't revolve or evolve the series but also doesn't do anything wrong so there is no reason to avoid it, unless you hate the kind of game it is.

Nintendo really likes bringing back old things just for nostalgia. However, I've never played a Mario&Luigi game before this one so I'm not gonna be driven by emotions. First things first, it looks really nice, I like the new artstyle, it's refreshing. The combat system is really fun and I find myself going towards enemies just to play battles. The story is great, I like the plot twist at the end and it's overall a pretty good story for a Mario game. I wanted togive Mario & Luigi: Brothership a 9, but I think that towards the end of the game the pacing starts to slow down and you get bored of esploring and just playing. All in all, I think it's one of the best Mario RPG experiences you can buy on the Switch thanks to its presentation, story and beatiful mechanics

It's been overwhelming. I liked the visuals and most of the gameplay but the game is too unbalanced and drawn out in time for my likings. And that freaky song in EVERY battle... Could have used several different ones...

The beginning is amazing, the middle of it is tiring and then the ending goes back to amazing. I liked the story and connected to the characters, but midgame has some really boring moments. Still recommend it though, I had a nice time with it!
Way too much hand-holding. This game is targeted to the same audience as Dora the Explorer, and fails even with them because no one on earth wants to read endless pointless dialogue.

Brothership is a solid entry into the Mario & Luigi series with fun combat, an intriguing (if somewhat contrived) storyline, and relatively satisfying boss battles and secret-hunting elements. However, it lacks the strong, idiosyncratic humor from previous iterations like "Partners In Time" or "Bowser's Inside Story", the difficulty level leaves a lot to be desired, and there is little-to-no incentive to replay the game. 

I did not take any damage until the first boss, and I did not have either of the Bros faint until 12-13 hours into the game, which was easily remedied by one of the dozen-or-so 1-UPs I had accumulated thus far. I didn't once get a "GAME OVER" during my playthrough. Mushrooms were more of a sellable commodity than a useful mid-battle heal, and the defensive gear options seemed inconsequential when I wasn't taking any hits in the first place. I understand this may not have been the typical experience, but, at least for me, the game felt way too easy. It's okay to make the game accessible to fans that are less experienced with RPGs, some difficulty options (sliders, toggles, really ANYTHING) for veterans like myself would have been welcomed with open arms.

The story this time around was acceptable, but little more than that. It also felt somewhat contrived, and it crucially lacked some of the punchy, oddball humor that other games in the series had plenty of. The key side characters felt more like plot devices than actual living, breathing elements that genuinely interacted with the Bros, and the world was little more than a series of locations designed to advance the story. Each new island offered little in the way of unique storytelling elements; one by one, Mario and Luigi would travel there, plug in the giant plug, and leave. Very few of the "levels" left a lasting impact on me, and I felt no desire (outside of the requirement for sidequests) to revisit any of them.

All this to say, the game ISN'T BAD. Likewise, however, it's not much more than DECENT. In some ways, it feels half-baked (particularly the story and character development), and I don't see myself coming back to this one a couple of years down the road to revisit the story or re-explore the islands. Fans had to wait a long time for this game, and it's a bit disappointing that Nintendo couldn't deliver a more fulfilling, engaging experience. Add a difficulty slider or hard mode, and I'd be all over it, but until then I won't be returning to Concordia any time soon. I'd give it a 7.4 if I could, but I suppose a 7 is closer to what I'm feeling here than an 8.
Boring game and a lot talking make the game more boring the gameplay in not that bad i dont like the style of gameplay

Luigi does way to much and it's shoved into your face whenever luigi has something he can do. the character designs are all bland and boring and all look the same.  leveling up is something that just happens and it doesn't feel like your getting that much stronger. it also never feels like you actually have to but any items at all. the battle system has way to much unnecessary stuff thrown into it. they decided to go for quantity over quality for all the islands and it shows. it feels like nintendo was trying to use the hype and another mario and luigi game to sell this one but it just didn't work. IGN was right.
A game that is as long as is equally boring. The best part is the few mario rpg aspects of battle. Most missions are strung out far too long for no real reason the game is not really fun.
Boring game and a lot talking make the game more boring the gameplay in not that bad i dont like the style of gameplay
